title: Setting Up and Connecting to the Company VPN on macOS
text:
Q: How do I set up and connect to the company VPN on my Mac?
A: Step 1: Click on the Apple menu in the top-left corner of your screen and select 'System Preferences'. Step 2: In the System Preferences window, click on 'Network'. Step 3: In the Network window, click the '+' button at the bottom left to add a new network connection. Step 4: From the 'Interface' dropdown menu, select 'VPN'. Step 5: Choose the VPN type (usually 'L2TP over IPSec' or 'Cisco IPSec') from the 'VPN Type' dropdown, and give your VPN a name in the 'Service Name' field. Step 6: Click 'Create'. Step 7: Enter the VPN server address provided by your IT department in the 'Server Address' field and your username in the 'Account Name' field. Step 8: Click on 'Authentication Settings', enter your password and shared secret (if required), then click 'OK'. Step 9: Click 'Apply' to save your settings. Step 10: To connect, select the VPN from the list and click 'Connect'. You should now be connected to the company VPN.
